description: Mobile-first Sudoku 9×9 logic puzzle. Tap a cell to select; tap a number 1-9 on the on-screen pad to fill (or use keyboard on desktop). Pencil-mark notes mode for tracking candidates. Real-time conflict highlighting (red), same-value highlighting (yellow), and row/column/3×3-subgrid highlighting (soft blue) when a cell is selected. 4 difficulty modes: easy/medium/hard/expert (40/32/26/22 clues). Procedurally-generated puzzles via the Lego — no hardcoded puzzle list. Fully Lego-composed: imports entities.sudoku-board verbatim. Touch-only via pointerup, dark-mode aware, mob-001..016 mobile wisdom applied.
qualityNotes: Hand-crafted Lego-composed reference for recipe.sudoku. Demonstrates: (1) inline source-copy of GridBoard + SudokuBoard (verbatim, marker present), (2) procedural puzzle generation via SudokuBoard.generatePuzzle(difficulty), (3) 3×3 subgrid borders via right-thick / bottom-thick CSS classes (avoids the 'flat 9×9 grid' look), (4) cell-selected highlighting cascade: same row + same col + same 3×3 subgrid all softly highlighted, plus same-value cells highlighted yellow (huge UX boost), (5) real-time conflict-set computation via SudokuBoard.allConflicts(), (6) pencil-mark notes mode toggle (3×3 mini-grid of candidates inside each cell), (7) on-screen 1-9 pad + keyboard support (number keys, Backspace, N for notes), (8) 4 difficulty modes with auto-restart, (9) timer + error counter, (10) end-game overlay with stats, (11) dark-mode aware via prefers-color-scheme. Use as the seed when matcher picks recipe.sudoku.

discriminator.reasoning: Hand-crafted by platform team to fill the Sudoku genre gap (zero coverage before this commit). Composes recipe.sudoku's required Lego (entities.sudoku-board). Mobile-first portrait full-bleed with all mobile-frontend wisdom rules applied. AntiKeywords for minesweeper/crossword/wordle disambiguate from neighboring grid-puzzle genres.
